threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look up at the bus when it pulls over, like it's a friendly giant. Everyone else just glances at their phones and walks past.
Saw a guy on the 59 singing to his reflection in the window, completely oblivious. The moonlight made it look like a late-night karaoke bar.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a café with a 'No WiFi, talk to each other' sign, I realized Neukölln had its own vibe, fiercely resisting the digital noise.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous techno parties in backyards, where neighbors just turn up with speakers and everyone dances until sunrise.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they’ll complain about a BART delay but still ride the 49 with a smile, not batting an eye at the packed bus.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a guy in a three-piece suit juggling on the bus while his coffee nearly spilt—only in SF do morning commutes come with a side of street performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every Catalan speaks Spanish; many here prefer Català. You can feel the pride in the way locals switch languages seamlessly.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their confused look at the tram stops, still unsure how to decode the weird routes and timings.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'the Tube' instead of 'the underground' and get confused by locals calling it 'the Overground' like it’s a completely different thing.
Last night on the 133, a group was debating whether the bus driver secretly judges them based on their snacks. One guy swore he changed his choice to avoid embarrassment.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the old Turkish grocer haggle over a pomegranate, while the young hipster in the corner sipped kombucha. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you catch a spontaneous kite festival on a Wednesday afternoon at Tempelhofer Feld while the buses are on strike.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to bring earplugs during a BART delay; the shrieks of techies confused by transit are a dead giveaway.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a kid using an old Muni transfer as a fan, the kind that should've been thrown out ages ago. Just another day in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think the local bars serve only vermut, but many still pour the best cañas you’ll find in the city — if you know where to look.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still studying the metro map while standing in the middle of the station, looking utterly baffled by the Tic Tac time displays.
